meta.description: NbViewer Android project state file. Load this at the start of any new development session to restore full architectural context without re-derivation.
meta.session_notes: M1 fully implemented. All files compile. Stub repository returns 3-cell notebook. ViewModel guards against concurrent loads. SAF picker uses OpenDocument with */* MIME. Intent filter covers 3 MIME types for open-with. repeatOnLifecycle(STARTED) used for safe StateFlow collection. No new dependencies introduced beyond what architecture defined.

product.name: NbViewer
product.tagline: Offline Jupyter Notebook viewer for Android
product.purpose: Read and display .ipynb files locally on-device. No code execution. No network access. No bundled runtimes.
non_goals_v1: Rendering HTML outputs from notebook cells
Rendering image outputs (placeholder text shown instead)
Hilt or other DI framework (manual DI only)

principles: Domain layer has zero Android SDK imports — pure Kotlin only
Each layer communicates with the layer below via interfaces only
Presentation layer consumes UI models, never domain models directly
All file I/O occurs on Dispatchers.IO, never the main thread
ViewModel is the single source of truth for UI state via StateFlow

responsibilities: JSON deserialization of .ipynb files into DTOs
Converting domain models to CellUiModel list

di.approach: Manual DI via AppContainer singleton in Application class
di.rationale: Zero overhead, zero magic, zero APK cost. Upgrade to Hilt when module count justifies it.
di.upgrade_trigger: When the object graph exceeds 3 feature modules or scoping becomes complex

async.io_dispatcher: Dispatchers.IO for all file reads and parsing
async.ui_dispatcher: Main thread only for StateFlow collection and view updates

navigation.graph: nav_graph.xml with HomeFragment as start destination

dependencies.policy: Minimal, well-justified, no transitive bloat. Every dependency must be re-justified at each major version.

libraries.justification: Zero-reflection compiler plugin approach. Smaller than Gson, Kotlin-native, ignoreUnknownKeys ensures forward-compat with future nbformat versions.
libraries.alternative_considered: Moshi — acceptable but requires kapt or KSP; Gson — heavier, reflection-based

libraries.purpose: Render Markdown cells to Spannable without WebView
libraries.justification: Only viable Android Markdown renderer that produces native Spannables applied to TextView. No WebView, no JS, small footprint.
libraries.alternative_considered: WebView — explicitly prohibited. CommonMark-Java — produces HTML, still needs a renderer.
None — this is the Android standard

libraries.purpose: Coroutine dispatcher for background I/O and main thread dispatch
libraries.justification: Required for structured concurrency. Dispatchers.IO for file reads prevents UI blocking.

libraries.purpose: Efficient scrolling list of heterogeneous cell types
libraries.justification: Industry standard, required for multi-viewtype cell list with DiffUtil.
libraries.alternative_considered: Jetpack Compose LazyColumn — viable future upgrade but increases APK by ~2MB for v1

libraries.purpose: registerForActivityResult API for SAF file picker
libraries.justification: Provides lifecycle-safe ActivityResultLauncher. Mandatory for SAF integration on API 26+.
libraries.alternative_considered: None — deprecated startActivityForResult cannot be used

libraries.justification: Safe args + back stack management. Minimal size.
libraries.alternative_considered: Manual FragmentTransaction — feasible but error-prone for back stack

Notebook.notes: Top-level model. Language resolved from kernelspec.language → language_info.name → 'python'

Cell.notes: id is index-based in v1 ('cell_0', 'cell_1'). Future: use nbformat 4.5 'id' field.

NotebookSource.notes: UriSource wraps android.net.Uri.toString() so domain stays Android-free

LoadNotebookUseCase.description: Delegates to INotebookRepository. Single public operator fun invoke().

M1.title: File Selection + Safe URI Handling
M1.description: SAF file picker, URI passed to ViewModel via NotebookSource.UriSource, lifecycle-safe ActivityResultLauncher, error path for null/invalid URI, no UI blocking.
deliverables: MainActivity.kt — single-activity host with NavController + intent handling ✓
nav_graph.xml — navigation graph with HomeFragment start + sourceUriString arg ✓
AppContainer.kt — manual DI wiring (stub repo until M3) ✓
StubNotebookRepository.kt — compile-time stub returns 3-cell notebook for testing ✓
AndroidManifest.xml — intent-filter for .ipynb open-with (3 MIME types) ✓
DomainModels.kt — Notebook, Cell, CellOutput, NotebookMetadata sealed classes ✓
NotebookViewerViewModel.kt — StateFlow + load guard + domain→UI mapping ✓
ViewerState.kt — NotebookUiState, CellUiModel, OutputUiModel sealed classes ✓
M1.verification: Install on device/emulator. Tap Open File → system picker appears → select any file → ViewModel emits Loading then Success → Logcat shows 'Loaded 3 cells successfully' (stub). Rotate device → no re-load occurs. Open .ipynb via file manager → app launches directly to viewer.

M2.title: JSON Parsing Pipeline Validation
M2.description: NotebookDto, NotebookParser, FileDataSource, unit tests with real .ipynb fixture files.
deliverables: ParserTest.kt with 3 fixture .ipynb files (simple, complex, malformed)

M3.title: Domain Mapping Verification
M3.description: NotebookDtoMapper, domain models, INotebookRepository, LoadNotebookUseCase, full pipeline test.

M4.title: RecyclerView Rendering (Unstyled)
M4.description: NotebookAdapter with DiffUtil, three ViewHolder types, ViewModel wired to fragment, unstyled but functional cell list.

M5.title: Markdown + Code Rendering Integration
M5.description: Markwon integration in MarkdownCellViewHolder, SyntaxHighlighter wired into CodeCellViewHolder, output rendering, ProGuard rules.

adrs.title: Manual DI over Hilt
adrs.context: App has one feature and a small object graph in v1.
adrs.decision: Use AppContainer with manual wiring. Zero overhead, zero annotation processing.
adrs.consequences: If object graph grows beyond 3 feature modules, migrate to Hilt. Migration is mechanical — interfaces don't change.

adrs.title: kotlinx.serialization over Gson/Moshi
adrs.context: Need JSON parsing. Gson uses reflection. Moshi needs KSP/kapt.
adrs.consequences: Forward-compat with nbformat 5+. Requires kotlin serialization gradle plugin.

adrs.title: Markwon over WebView for Markdown
adrs.context: Markdown cells must render offline with no WebView.
adrs.decision: io.noties.markwon:markwon-core renders Markdown as native Android Spannables on a TextView.
adrs.consequences: Complex Markdown edge cases (tables, footnotes) need Markwon extension modules. Add per-need, not upfront.

adrs.title: Custom span-based syntax highlighter
adrs.context: No third-party code view library that is small, maintained, and WebView-free.
adrs.decision: Custom SyntaxHighlighter using regex patterns + ForegroundColorSpan applied to SpannableString.
adrs.consequences: Python coverage only in v1. Adding new languages is O(1) — append a pattern set.

adrs.title: SAF (Storage Access Framework) for file access
adrs.context: READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE is deprecated on API 33+. Direct path access unreliable across OEMs.
adrs.decision: Use ActivityResultContracts.OpenDocument(). Works on all API 26+ without storage permission.
adrs.consequences: URI persistence requires takePersistableUriPermission if re-open after process death is needed (future feature).

adrs.title: NotebookSource abstraction at domain boundary
adrs.context: Domain layer must not import android.net.Uri.
adrs.decision: NotebookSource.UriSource(uriString: String) stores Uri.toString(). Reconstructed in data layer.
adrs.consequences: Domain is 100% JVM-testable. Uri parsing stays in FileDataSource.

adrs.title: Index-based cell IDs in v1
adrs.context: nbformat 4.5 added an 'id' field per cell. Older notebooks don't have it.
adrs.decision: Generate IDs as 'cell_{index}' during mapping. Stable within a single load.
adrs.consequences: DiffUtil animations work correctly. If nbformat id field is exposed in future, replace during mapper update only.

adrs.title: RecyclerView over Jetpack Compose
adrs.context: Target devices include low-end hardware. Compose adds ~2MB to APK and has higher initial frame costs.
adrs.decision: RecyclerView with ViewHolder pattern and DiffUtil.
adrs.consequences: Future migration to Compose LazyColumn is feasible — adapters can be replaced without touching domain or data layers.

extension_points.seam: Add FileHistoryRepository (backed by SharedPreferences or Room) to AppContainer. New UseCase. HomeFragment gets new UI section. Zero existing code changes.
SearchNotebookUseCase accepts Notebook + query string, returns List<SearchResult>. ViewModel exposes search StateFlow alongside existing cells StateFlow.
CellOutput.DisplayData already in domain model. OutputUiModel.ImageOutput added to sealed class. New ImageOutputViewHolder inside CodeCellViewHolder's output section.
SyntaxHighlighter already accepts a Map<TokenType, Int> color map. Wire to current theme resource at bind time.
ExportNotebookUseCase consumes existing Notebook domain model. No parsing changes needed.
Wrap NotebookRepositoryImpl with CachingNotebookRepository decorator. INotebookRepository interface unchanged.
Cell.Unknown already handles unrecognized cell_type. Mapper extended with new when branch. DTOs already ignore unknown fields.
Replace AppContainer with @Module classes. All constructor signatures are already injection-ready.
ViewModel + StateFlow + domain layer are Compose-compatible today. Only ViewHolder/Adapter code is replaced.
Markwon has a math extension module. Add dependency + register plugin in Markwon builder in MarkdownCellViewHolder.

unit.scope: Domain + Data layers. Pure JVM, no Android dependencies.

integration.scope: Repository + Parser with real .ipynb fixture files as test resources

ui.scope: Fragment navigation, RecyclerView item count, error state display
ui.deferred_reason: No UI to test until M4

constraints_log: Must work fully offline — no network calls anywhere in codebase
Must NOT bundle Python, Jupyter, Node.js, or any runtime
RAM target: under 50MB peak for typical notebook (< 500 cells)
No code execution — SyntaxHighlighter reads source as static text only
Domain layer: zero Android SDK imports enforced by package convention
